compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Finding (A1 — verified)
The workflow visual editor interpolates repo-sourced content into an inline `<script>` block via `json.dumps`, which does not escape `/`. Content containing a literal `</script>` breaks out of the script context and injects markup that runs with the in-page `window.REBAR_TOKEN` guarding the `/save` write endpoint.

Citations: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py:292-298` (json.dumps of bpmn_xml, prompts, contracts, library, descriptor, overlay_triggers) -> `:366-373` (interpolated into `<script>window.REBAR_* = {...}`). Untrusted inputs on a cloned/shared repo: `editor_server.py:159,162` (prompts via `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`), `:188` (enumerate_library), and the edited workflow YAML.

## Reproduction Steps
1. In a cloned repo, put `</script><script>fetch('/save',{method:'POST',body:...})</script>` inside a `.rebar/prompts/*.md` or workflow YAML.
2. Open the visual editor on that repo.
3. Expected: content rendered inert. Actual: injected script executes with REBAR_TOKEN, forging authenticated /save writes.

## Remediation
Add a `_js_embed(obj)` helper: `json.dumps(...)` then escape `<` (e.g. `</`->`<\/`, and `<!--`/`-->` defensively); route all interpolated payloads through it. (Alternative: move data into `<script type="application/json">` read via textContent.)

End state: repo-sourced strings cannot terminate the script element; REBAR_TOKEN no longer exfiltratable via crafted content.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All `json.dumps` payloads embedded into inline `<script>` in editor.py are escaped against `</script>` breakout
- [ ] A test asserts a payload containing `</script>` renders inert
- [ ] No behavior change to legitimate editor content
compiled_state.title: Fix editor XSS: escape </script> breakout in inline-script interpolation
